target.performance_gate: beat upstream baseline in same-pool live A/B windows

acceptance_gates: real pool accepted share is observed
rejected share count stays at zero during the test window
duplicate share count does not dominate the window
same backend, same pool, same power envelope, and same measurement window
no wallet address, worker name, host path, or machine identifier in committed artifacts

baselines.summary: Prior local live window showed about 109.94 TH/s average with accepted shares and no rejects.
baselines.lesson: Runtime carveout changes alone did not create a new throughput tier.

candidates.goal: Build the smallest backend that can satisfy the PlainProof/CAPI contract.
candidates.why: A slow correct backend gives us an owned test harness before optimizing.
gates: real pool accepted share is observed

candidates.goal: Change shared-memory layout or fragment materialization so the hot path is not the same SASS as prior wrapper variants.
candidates.why: Prior wrapper-level changes often compiled to identical SASS; the next attempt must affect the actual hot path.

candidates.goal: Shorten register lifetime around GEMM/transcript snapshot without changing proof semantics.
candidates.why: Prior evidence points at register pressure and ldmatrix/MMA scheduling as likely bottlenecks.

candidates.goal: Record a prior proof-valid small positive signal for future comparison.
candidates.why: Useful evidence, but below the promotion threshold for a public claim.
